  df_mod_ind_C2P <- rbind(df_mod_ind_C2P, df_mod_ind_i)
}

df_mod_Weight_ind_C2P <- subset(df_mod_ind_C2P, Endpt == "Weight" & Time !=0)
df_mod_CiEPX_ind_C2P <- subset(df_mod_ind_C2P, Endpt == "CiEPX" & Time !=0)

df_predobs_CiEPX_ind_C2P <- dObs_ind |> 
  filter(Time !=0) |> 
  left_join(df_mod_CiEPX_ind_C2P, suffix = c("_obs", "_pred")) |> 
  dplyr::select(-c(Weight_obs, CiIMD)) |> 
  mutate(across(everything(), ~replace(.x, .x == -1, NA))) |> 
  drop_na()

df_predobs_Weight_ind_C2P <- dObs_ind |> 
  filter(Time !=0) |> 
  left_join(df_mod_Weight_ind_C2P, suffix = c("_obs", "_pred"))|>
  dplyr::select(-c(CiEPX_obs, CiIMD)) |> 
  mutate(across(everything(), ~replace(.x, .x == -1, NA))) |>

lim_max <- max(df_predobs_Weight_ind_C1$predict.endpoint, df_predobs_Weight_ind_C1$Weight)
seq_line <- seq(lim_min, lim_max, 0.001)

df_line <- data.frame(
  x1  = seq_line,
  y1  = seq_line*fact_1,
  x_1 = seq_line,
  y_1 = seq_line/fact_1,

  x_2 = seq_line,
  y_2 = seq_line/fact_2
  )

p_W <- ggplot(
  data = df_predobs_Weight_ind_C1,
  mapping = aes(
      x = Weight_obs,
      y = predict.endpoint
    )
  )+
  geom_line(
    data=df_line,
    mapping=aes(x=x1, y=y1),
    color = col_line1
    )+
  geom_line(
    data=df_line,
    mapping=aes(x=x_1, y=y_1),
    color = col_line1
    )+
  geom_line(
    data=df_line,
    mapping=aes(x=x2, y=y2),
    color = col_line2
    )+

    mapping=aes(x=x_2, y=y_2),
    color = col_line2

    color = col_line0,
    linewidth = 1

      ymax = up

    ymax = max(df_predobs_Weight_ind_C1$predict.endpoint)
  )+
  scale_x_log10(limits=c(lim_min, lim_max))+
  scale_y_log10(limits=c(lim_min, lim_max))+
  labs(
    x = "Observed weight of earthworms (g)",
    y = "Predicted weight of earthworms (g)"
  )+
  theme_minimal()

dObs_ind <- df_TK_EPX |> 
  mutate(ID = as.numeric(ID))
colnames(dObs_ind)[colnames(dObs_ind) == "Weight"] <- "Weight_obs"
colnames(dObs_ind)[colnames(dObs_ind) == "CiEPX"] <- "CiEPX_obs"
colnames(dObs_ind)[colnames(dObs_ind) == "t"] <- "Times"

time_limits <- dObs_ind %>%
  group_by(ID) %>%
  summarise(time_cutoff = max(Time, na.rm = TRUE) + 5)

Time_points_Nb_rep <- dObs_ind |> 
  dplyr::select(ID, Time_point_f, Nb_rep)

y_segment <- 750

df_phases <- dObs_ind |>
  arrange(ID, Time) |>
  group_by(ID) |>
  mutate(
    row = row_number(),
    t_start = Time,
    t_end = lead(Time),  
    segment = row,
    y = y_segment
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(t_end)) |>  
  dplyr::select(ID, segment, t_start, t_end, y, Experiment) |>
  ungroup() |> 
  mutate(
    Phase = case_when(
      (segment == 1 & ID < 65) ~ 1,
      (segment == 2 & ID < 65) ~ 3,
      (segment == 1 & ID > 64) ~ 1,
      (segment == 2 & ID > 64) ~ 2,
      (segment == 3 & ID > 64) ~ 3,
      (Experiment == "TKBIS" & segment == 1)~1,
      (Experiment == "TKBIS" & segment == 2)~3
    )
  )

##| cache: false

#nb_rows_ind_C1 <- NC_C1*N_iter_setpoint_C1+1

########## C1 #############################################

Sim.Res.ind.full_C1 <- lapply(1:Nb_experiment_C1, function(j) {
  read_tsv(file.path(path_mod_EPX_C1, paste0("Setpoints_ind_full_", j, ".out"))) %>%
    mutate(sim = j)
}) %>%
  bind_rows()

df_mod_ind_C1 <- NULL

for (i in 1:Nb_experiment_C1){
  
  ID_i <- unique(dObs_ind$ID)[i]

  Sim.Res.ind.full_i  <- subset(Sim.Res.ind.full_C1, sim == i) |> 
    dplyr::select(where(~ !all(is.na(.x))))
  
  dObs_i <- subset(dObs_ind, ID == ID_i)
  
  Nom_Endpoints = c("Weight", "CiEPX", "C_exposure")
  
  Nsortie = length(Nom_Endpoints)
  
  MPV = IC_min = IC_max = Endpoint = index = NULL
  
  for (j in 1:length(Nom_Endpoints)){
    
    index =grep(paste0("^",Nom_Endpoints[j],"_") , colnames(Sim.Res.ind.full_i), fixed=FALSE)

    
    Data_sim = as.matrix( Sim.Res.ind.full_i[ , index] )
    
    MPV    = c(  MPV,  as.numeric( Data_sim[nrow(Data_sim), ] ) )
    IC_min = c(IC_min,  apply(Data_sim, 2, quantile,  0.025, na.rm=TRUE) )
    IC_max = c(IC_max,  apply(Data_sim, 2, quantile,  0.975, na.rm=TRUE) ) 
    
    Endpoint = c(Endpoint, rep(Nom_Endpoints[j], ncol(Data_sim)))
  }
  
  df_mod_ind_i <- data.frame(
    predict.endpoint = MPV,
    Time    = rep(Time_pred, Nsortie),
    low     = IC_min,
    up      = IC_max,
    Endpt   = Endpoint
    )
  
  df_mod_ind_i <- df_mod_ind_i %>%
    mutate(ID=ID_i)

  df_mod_ind_C1 <- rbind(df_mod_ind_C1, df_mod_ind_i)
}

########## C2P #############################################

Sim.Res.ind.full_C2P <- lapply(1:Nb_experiment_C2P, function(j) {
  read_tsv(file.path(path_mod_EPX_C2P, paste0("Setpoints_ind_full_", j, ".out"))) %>%
    mutate(sim = j)
}) %>%
  bind_rows()

df_mod_ind_C2P <- NULL

for (i in 1:Nb_experiment_C2P){
  
  ID_i <- unique(dObs_ind$ID)[i]

  Sim.Res.ind.full_i  <- subset(Sim.Res.ind.full_C2P, sim == i) |> 
    dplyr::select(where(~ !all(is.na(.x))))
  
  dObs_i <- subset(dObs_ind, ID == ID_i)
  
  Nom_Endpoints = c("Weight", "CiEPX", "C_exposure")
  
  Nsortie = length(Nom_Endpoints)
  
  MPV = IC_min = IC_max = Endpoint = index = NULL
  
  for (j in 1:length(Nom_Endpoints)){
    
    index =grep(paste0("^",Nom_Endpoints[j],"_") , colnames(Sim.Res.ind.full_i), fixed=FALSE)

    
    Data_sim = as.matrix( Sim.Res.ind.full_i[ , index] )
    
    MPV    = c(  MPV,  as.numeric( Data_sim[nrow(Data_sim), ] ) )
    IC_min = c(IC_min,  apply(Data_sim, 2, quantile,  0.025, na.rm=TRUE) )
    IC_max = c(IC_max,  apply(Data_sim, 2, quantile,  0.975, na.rm=TRUE) ) 
    
    Endpoint = c(Endpoint, rep(Nom_Endpoints[j], ncol(Data_sim)))
  }
  
  df_mod_ind_i <- data.frame(
    predict.endpoint = MPV,
    Time    = rep(Time_pred, Nsortie),
    low     = IC_min,
    up      = IC_max,
    Endpt   = Endpoint
    )
  
  df_mod_ind_i <- df_mod_ind_i %>%
    mutate(ID=ID_i)

  df_mod_ind_C2P <- rbind(df_mod_ind_C2P, df_mod_ind_i)
}

df_mod_CiEPX_ind_C2P <- subset(df_mod_ind_C2P, Endpt == "CiEPX")
df_mod_CeEPX_ind_C2P <- subset(df_mod_ind_C2P, Endpt == "C_exposure")

    data = df_phases_plot,

      xend = t_end,
      y = y,
      yend = y,
      group = ID,
      color = factor(Phase)
    ),
    linewidth = 2,
    alpha = 0.7
  ) +

  geom_line(
    data=df_mod_CiEPX_ind_t_plot_C1,

      y= low,

    color = col_EPX
    )+
  geom_line(
    data=df_mod_CiEPX_ind_t_plot_C1,
    aes(
      x=Time,
      y= up,
      group = ID
      ),
    linetype="dotted",
    color = col_EPX
    )+
  geom_line(
    data=df_mod_CiEPX_ind_t_plot_C1,
    aes(
      x=Time,
      y= predict.endpoint,
      group = ID
      ),
    color = col_EPX,
    lwd=1
    )+
  geom_ribbon(
    data=df_mod_CiEPX_ind_t_plot_C1,

      group = ID
      ),

      x = Time,
      y = CiEPX_obs
    ),
    size = 2,
    alpha = 0.7,
    color = col_red
  )+

    values = col_exposure,
    label = c("Uptake", "Elimination", "Depuration"),
    name = "Phase"
    )+
  labs(
    x = "Time (days)",
    y = "CiEPX (ng/g)"
  )+
  #lims(y=c(0, 300))+
  theme_minimal()

5.2.8 Simulation of the toxicodynamic in earthworms throughout the exposition
The goal here is to recreate the concentration dynamic in the average earthworm during our exposition scheme but without the fasting phase that was required for chemical analysis and during which elimination was happening.


Code

Time_max <- 42
Time_step <- 0.25

############ C1 ###########################################
Sim.Res_C1 <- as.data.frame(fread(here::here(path_mod_EPX_C1, "Setpoints.out"),sep="\t"))
  
  Nom_Endpoints =c("Weight_1", "CiEPX_1", "C_exposure_1")
  Nsortie = 3
  MPV = IC_min = IC_max = Endpoint = index = NULL

  for (i in 1:length(Nom_Endpoints)){
    
    index =grep(paste("^",Nom_Endpoints[i],"\\.", sep="") , colnames(Sim.Res_C1), fixed=FALSE )
    
    Data_sim = as.matrix( Sim.Res_C1[ , index] )
    
    MPV    = c(  MPV,  as.numeric( Data_sim[nrow(Data_sim), ] ) )
    IC_min = c(IC_min,  apply(Data_sim, 2, quantile,  0.025) )
    IC_max = c(IC_max,  apply(Data_sim, 2, quantile,  0.975) ) 
    
    Endpoint = c(Endpoint, rep(Nom_Endpoints[i], ncol(Data_sim)))

    }

  df_mod_C1 <- data.frame(
    predict.endpoint = MPV,
    Time    = c( rep(Time_pred, Nsortie)),
    low     = IC_min,
    up      = IC_max,
    Endpt   = Endpoint,
    Experiment = c(rep(rep("Experience 1", length(Time_pred)), Nsortie))
    )
  
df_mod_Weight_C1 <- subset(df_mod_C1, Endpt == "Weight_1")

title: Toxicokinetics of the mixture
text: This part is about the investigation of potential toxicokinetic interactions between imidaclorpid and epoxiconazole through the comparison of mixture experimental data and simulations with the toxicokinetic models implemented for single substances.

title: 7  Is there an interaction between IMD & EPX at the TK level ?
text:
7.1 Data importation
We want to answer the following question : Can we predict the concentrations in EPX and IMD in earthworms exposed to different ratios of the mixture ?
Code
df_TK_mix <- f_read_data_TK_mix() |>
  mutate(
    Ratio_plot = case_when(
      Ratio == "E" ~ "1:0 (EPX only)",
      Ratio == "F" ~ "3:1",
      Ratio == "G" ~ "1:1",
      Ratio == "H" ~ "1:3",
      Ratio == "I" ~ "0:1 (IMD only)",
      Ratio == "N" ~ "Ctrl"
    ),
    Ratio_plot = factor(Ratio_plot, levels = Ratio_plot[!duplicated(Ratio)])
  )

Ci0IMD <- mean(subset(df_TK_mix, Ratio == "E")$CiIMD, na.rm = T)
Ci0EPX <- mean(subset(df_TK_mix, Ratio == "I")$CiEPX, na.rm = T)

df_TK_mix_mean_Ce <- subset(df_TK_mix, t = 0) |>
  group_by(Ratio, Line) |>
  mutate(
    CeIMD = mean(CeIMD, na.rm = T),
    CeEPX = mean(CeEPX, na.rm = T)
  ) |> 
  dplyr::select(CeIMD, CeEPX) |> 
  unique()

df_TK_mix_mean <- subset(df_TK_mix, t > 28) |>
  dplyr::select(-c(CeIMD, CeEPX)) |> 
  group_by(Ratio, Line) |>
  mutate(
    Dose_IMD = mean(Dose_IMD, na.rm = T),
    Dose_EPX = mean(Dose_EPX, na.rm = T)
  ) |> 
  ungroup() |> 
  left_join(df_TK_mix_mean_Ce, by = c("Ratio", "Line")) |> 
  group_by(Ratio, Line) |>
  mutate(
    BAFIMD_obs = mean(CiIMD / CeIMD, na.rm = T),
    BAFEPX_obs = mean(CiEPX / CeEPX, na.rm = T)
  ) |> 
  ungroup()

title: 7  Is there an interaction between IMD & EPX at the TK level ?
section: 7.2 Quantity in earthworms vs. quantity in the cosm
text:
7.2 Quantity in earthworms vs. quantity in the cosm


Code
df_TK_mix_Qtity <- df_TK_mix_mean |> 
  mutate(
    Q_EPX_ew = CiEPX * Weight, # ng/g x g
    Q_IMD_ew = CiIMD * Weight,
    Q_EPX_cosm = CeEPX * 200, # ng/g x 200
    Q_IMD_cosm = CeIMD * 200,
    R_Q_EPX_ew_cosm = Q_EPX_ew / Q_EPX_cosm * 100,
    R_Q_IMD_ew_cosm = Q_IMD_ew / Q_IMD_cosm * 100
  )

p <- ggplot(
  data = df_TK_mix_Qtity,
  aes(
    x = paste0(Ratio, Line)
  )
)+
  geom_point(
    mapping = aes(
      y = R_Q_EPX_ew_cosm
    ),
    color = col_EPX,
    alpha = 0.5,
    size = 3
  )+
  geom_point(
    mapping = aes(
      y = R_Q_IMD_ew_cosm
    ),
    color = col_IMD,
    alpha = 0.5,
    size = 3

title: 8  Conclusion
text:
This study provides a detailed assessment of the toxicokinetics of two pesticides, epoxiconazole and imidacloprid, in earthworms (Aporrectodea caliginosa), both as single substances and in mixtures. The two compounds exhibited strikingly different kinetic behaviors: imidacloprid, despite its hydrophilic nature, accumulated slowly and was eliminated very slowly, whereas epoxiconazole, moderately lipophilic, reached equilibrium rapidly and was efficiently eliminated. These patterns highlight that bioaccumulation potential cannot be reliably predicted from log \(K_{ow}\) or \(K_{oc}\) alone, and that compound-specific physiological and soil-organism interactions play a critical role.
The use of two-compartment toxicokinetic models allowed a more mechanistic representation of internal distribution and redistribution dynamics, capturing biphasic uptake and elimination for both substances. In particular, explicitly modeling the depuration phase on Petri dishes proved essential for accurate parameter estimation, especially for epoxiconazole. The mixture experiment revealed no detectable toxicokinetic interactions between epoxiconazole and imidacloprid, indicating that single-substance models can reliably describe uptake and elimination in this context.
Additionally, the study emphasizes the importance of considering concentration-dependent processes. At high exposure levels, both compounds showed signs of saturation, whether at the tissue level or in relation to soil organic matter binding, underscoring that internal concentrations may not scale linearly with external exposure. This finding questions the use of single, concentration-independent bioaccumulation factors for hazard assessment and highlights the value of integrated TKTD approaches.
Finally, our work demonstrates the importance of well-characterized experimental exposure scenarios and careful handling of soil organisms in toxicokinetic studies. Incorporating short-term depuration, gut-content dynamics, and variability in soil properties improves model reliability and the interpretation of bioaccumulation data. Overall, the combined use of mechanistic toxicokinetic models and controlled experimental designs provides a robust framework to understand and predict the uptake, distribution, and elimination of chemicals in soil invertebrates, with implications for both single substances and mixtures in environmental risk assessment.
